occurrence_sentence: Scientists have long struggled with a difficult trade-off in drug design. Only very slow, costly computations have allowed one to simulate molecular interactions with quantum-level accuracy, capturing the subtle dance of electrons and atoms. Though they depend on approximations that overlook important details, faster techniques exist. Now bridging this gap, an artificial intelligence model called FeNNix-Bio1 is providing practical speed and cost-effective quantum chemical accuracy. Combining the best of both worlds in molecular simulation, this breakthrough is poised to hasten drug discovery and change how we study molecules.

mentions.occurrence_sentence: Qubit Pharmaceuticals Announces Strategic Collaboration with Singapore's Centre for Quantum Technologies to Advance Quantum Algorithms for Drug Discovery
“Quantum algorithms for chemistry have been studied for decades, but real implementations remain rare,” said Robert Marino, CEO of Qubit Pharmaceuticals.

mentions.role_in_text: Collaborating with CQT to advance quantum algorithms for drug discovery
mentions.occurrence_sentence: The goal is to bring advanced quantum chemistry methods closer to real-world drug discovery applications. Together, the teams are designing and testing algorithms for quantum chemistry, including variational quantum eigensolvers, quantum phase estimation, and quantum Markov Chain Monte Carlo (qMCMC) sampling. These algorithms target key computational bottlenecks in drug discovery, such as improving the accuracy of quantum chemistry calculations for better drug property predictions and enabling more efficient sampling techniques for molecular simulations.

The project is supported by Singapore's National Quantum Computing Hub (NQCH), through which CQT researchers have access to run experiments on Quantinuum's Marino and Baptiste Claudon presented first results from these experiments on 23 April at a Quantum Industry Day in Singapore organised by Quantinuum and Singapore's National Quantum Office for some 250 invited participants.

mentions.role_in_text: Company involved in quantum algorithms for drug discovery
mentions.occurrence_sentence: "Drug discovery is fundamentally a molecular simulation challenge. If we can model chemistry with greater fidelity and efficiency, we can make better decisions earlier in the pipeline," said Piquemal, Chief Scientific Officer / Co-founder, Qubit Pharmaceuticals.
![Picture of Qubit Pharmaceuticals

mentions.role_in_text: Company involved in quantum algorithms for drug discovery

mentions.role_in_text: The company that published the blog post.
mentions.occurrence_sentence: Open main navigation Close main navigation

Scientists have long struggled with a difficult trade-off in drug design. Only very slow, costly computations have allowed one to simulate molecular interactions with quantum-level accuracy, capturing the subtle dance of electrons and atoms. Though they depend on approximations that overlook important details, faster techniques exist. Now bridging this gap, an artificial intelligence model called FeNNix-Bio1 is providing practical speed and cost-effective quantum chemical accuracy. Combining the best of both worlds in molecular simulation, this breakthrough is poised to hasten drug discovery and change how we study molecules.
[ Qubit Pharmaceuticals
Predicting how a protein folds or how a drug candidate will bind to its target in drug research boils down to physics and conventions have pushed researchers into a choice between speed and accuracy:
https://blog.qubit-pharmaceuticals.com/blog/quantum-accuracy-at-real-world-speed-fennix-bio1-ai-heralds-a-drug-discovery-leap
https://blog.qubit-pharmaceuticals.com/blog/quantum-accuracy-at-real-world-speed-fennix-bio1-ai-heralds-a-drug-discovery-leap
Despite its strong physics grounding, FeNNix-Bio1 runs blazingly fast.

In practical terms, that increased speed lets scientists simulate larger systems or longer timescales without sacrificing accuracy. FeNNix-Bio1 can scale up to simulate hundreds of thousands or even millions of atoms on modern GPUs, tackling systems as big as an entire virus's protein shell or a cellular membrane patch.
https://blog.qubit-pharmaceuticals.com/blog/quantum-accuracy-at-real-world-speed-fennix-bio1-ai-heralds-a-drug-discovery-leap
https://blog.qubit-pharmaceuticals.com/blog/quantum-accuracy-at-real-world-speed-fennix-bio1-ai-heralds-a-drug-discovery-leap
FeNNix-Bio1's development introduces an era where physics and AI can collaborate to solve challenges that were once thought impossible.

It's like a chemical flight simulator that flawlessly knows and applies the laws of physics, rather than aerodynamics, to empower researchers to test their ideas in a safe, quick and affordable digital environment without committing to the time, costs and safety risks associated with real-world trials.
![Picture of Qubit Pharmaceuticals

![Picture of Qubit Pharmaceuticals](https://blog.qubit-pharmaceuticals.com/hs-fs/hubfs/ICON_green-removebg-preview.png?width=50&name=ICON_green-removebg-preview.png) [Qubit Pharmaceuticals](https://blog.qubit-pharmaceuticals.com/blog/author/qubit-pharmaceuticals)

mentions.occurrence_sentence: Open main navigation Close main navigation

Qubit Pharmaceuticals accelerates and transforms drug discovery by publishing an unprecedentedly accurate computational approach combining their core technologies with exciting innovations: highest accuracy in molecular simulation thanks to next generation Polarizable Models and Tailored Free Energy Methods to predict the binding affinities between RNA and small molecules, the newly developed Lambda-ABF scheme and enhanced sampling techniques, including a Machine Learning approach.

mentions.role_in_text: Developing computational approaches for drug discovery
mentions.occurrence_sentence: Qubit Pharmaceuticals accelerates and transforms drug discovery by publishing an unprecedentedly accurate computational approach combining their core technologies with exciting innovations: highest accuracy in molecular simulation thanks to next generation Polarizable Models and Tailored Free Energy Methods to predict the binding affinities between RNA and small molecules, the newly developed Lambda-ABF scheme and enhanced sampling techniques, including a Machine Learning approach.
How Qubit Pharmaceuticals innovates to develop efficient RNA targeting pipelines
How Qubit Pharmaceuticals innovates to develop efficient RNA targeting pipelines

mentions.role_in_text: Developing advanced technologies and running drug discovery platform.
mentions.occurrence_sentence: By integrating advanced technologies, developed by Qubit Pharmaceuticals and their partners, like the Lambda-ABF method, and Tinker-HP Software, powered by Qubit Pharmaceuticals' 200 GPU supercomputing cluster, Gaia, and run on its proprietary drug discovery platform Atlas, we achieve unparalleled accuracy and speed in predicting RNA-small molecule interactions.

mentions.role_in_text: Developed the Lambda-ABF method and Tinker-HP Software, and operates the Gaia supercomputing cluster and Atlas drug discovery platform.
mentions.occurrence_sentence: By integrating advanced technologies, developed by Qubit Pharmaceuticals and their partners, like the Lambda-ABF method, and Tinker-HP Software, powered by Qubit Pharmaceuticals' 200 GPU supercomputing cluster, Gaia, and run on its proprietary drug discovery platform Atlas, we achieve unparalleled accuracy and speed in predicting RNA-small molecule interactions.

As pharmaceutical companies seek to develop effective treatments for diseases that currently lack viable options, our research provides the insights and tools necessary for precise drug discovery, ultimately guiding the way toward new breakthroughs in healthcare.

Traditional quantum algorithms often wander this landscape inefficiently or get stuck on plateaus, especially given today's imperfect hardware.

mentions.role_in_text: Developer of the greedy quantum algorithm
mentions.occurrence_sentence: This new approach not only uses far fewer quantum operations to reach accurate results, but it has also proven surprisingly robust to noise, even demonstrating its prowess on a real 25-qubit quantum computer.
https://blog.qubit-pharmaceuticals.com/blog/greedy-quantum-algorithm-charts-a-new-course-to-ground-state-energy-success-1
One popular approach for near-term hardware is the Variational Quantum Eigensolver (VQE).
https://blog.qubit-pharmaceuticals.com/blog/greedy-quantum-algorithm-charts-a-new-course-to-ground-state-energy-success-1
https://blog.qubit-pharmaceuticals.com/blog/greedy-quantum-algorithm-charts-a-new-course-to-ground-state-energy-success-1
In plainer terms, it's like knowing that a valley's cross-section is a smooth U-shape – a few soundings can tell you where the bottom is.

This greedy one-step-at-a-time construction yields a somewhat less flexible final circuit than if one re-optimized everything repeatedly, but it dramatically cuts down the required quantum evaluations and is far more NISQ-friendly.

In fact, the new algorithm needs only a fixed, small number of measurements for each iteration – the authors report just two-to-five circuit measurements per iteration, regardless of the number of qubits or operators considered.

mentions.role_in_text: The blog is from Qubit Pharmaceuticals, indicating they are involved in quantum computing research.
mentions.occurrence_sentence: https://blog.qubit-pharmaceuticals.com/blog/greedy-quantum-algorithm-charts-a-new-course-to-ground-state-energy-success-1
It highlights the algorithm's surprising noise-resilience: despite noisy energy readings, it can still zero in on the correct ground-state form.

The authors put it best. “This research brings key progress towards the full implementation of variational methods for quantum chemistry and proposes a first – real life – converged computation on an actual NISQ quantum computer”. In short, it's a proof-of-concept that real-world quantum advantage – using quantum hardware to solve useful chemistry problems better than classical methods – is getting closer.
https://blog.qubit-pharmaceuticals.com/blog/greedy-quantum-algorithm-charts-a-new-course-to-ground-state-energy-success-1
It's a _symbiotic strategy_ : the precision of quantum physics combined with the power of AI.

Biomemory, HyVibe, Qubit Pharmaceuticals, Tortoise and Welinq are the outstanding research and development companies chosen to join the Cité de l'Innovation de Sorbonne Université.
Biomemory, HyVibe, Qubit Pharmaceuticals, Tortoise and Welinq are the outstanding research and development companies chosen to join the Cité de l'Innovation de Sorbonne Université.

mentions.role_in_text: Hosts the Cité de l'innovation and supports startups and innovation.
mentions.occurrence_sentence: These start-ups, whose technologies originate from the university laboratories, have all presented, in their application and then during their hearing, a project of scientific cooperation and development of their relations with the student community of Sorbonne Université.

mentions.role_in_text: The Cité de l'Innovation de Sorbonne Université will enable research and development companies, created in Sorbonne Université laboratories or in connection with them, to develop in dedicated premises, thus creating an unprecedented link between university research and the business world.
mentions.occurrence_sentence: An unprecedented place that promotes scientific innovation and entrepreneurship The Cité de l'Innovation de Sorbonne Université will enable research and development companies, created in Sorbonne Université laboratories or in connection with them, to develop in dedicated premises, thus creating an unprecedented link between university research and the business world.
Start-ups from Sorbonne Université research laboratories have raised an impressive €1.5 billion over the past ten years.
